At some point, someone saw 倒数时刻 turn into "New Year Countdown" and gave it a green light.
I say we standardize to the latter.

Yeah, I really wasn't sure what to call the Wheel other than "literally anything other than this"
Now that it's been clarified that someone, somewhere, in BIAV did make a decision at some point to allow "New Year Countdown" to be an acceptable translation for the Wheel's name, thats what I'd like to go with.

I don't think Counting Down the Hours is any good, but it's the best I could come up with at the time, and debate brought us to better conclusions together
> someone, somewhere, in BIAV did make a decision at some point to allow "New Year Countdown" to be an acceptable translation for the Wheel's name

<@1376763322568937562> I'm working on a second pass on Wheels of Destiny and I wanted to address the clunky naming of the New Year's wheel and its buff card.

I put some thought into it and the best I can come up with is "**Counting Down the Hours**".
I'd also like to shorten the card's name to just "**Countdown: X**" so that it doesn't take so long to scroll down to the number at the end.

Here's my take on the wheel's description as well; I think going for an explanation of what the cards *do* is better here than a direct reading of what the cards *say,* considering it's a weird mechanic.

- **Counting Down the Hours**
> At the start of investigation, add the unique Buff card "Countdown: 5" to the deck.
> "Countdown" cards have 0 Arithmetica Cost and persist between battles. When one is played, __Destroy__ it, draw 1 card, and put the next "Countdown" in sequence into the Discard Pile.
> "Countdown: 0" has __Retain__. When it's played, __Destroy__ it, then instead draw 3 cards, gain 3 Arithmetica, and there's a `100%` chance to put "Countdown: 5" into the Discard Pile.
